records.population: Babies with bronchiolitis, generally under 12 months
verifiedBoundaries: Reviewed July 2025
Bronchiolitis-specific and not generalisable to every respiratory illness
Home observation depends on comfortable breathing and adequate feeding
Reviewed September 2025
Young children and babies are at increased dehydration risk
Severe dehydration requires urgent medical help
Last reviewed December 2024
Australian pathway
A baby under 3 months with temperature above 38 degrees Celsius requires immediate emergency-department assessment

records.population: Children exposed to first-generation oral sedating antihistamines
verifiedBoundaries: Published 13 July 2022
Do not use for cough, cold and flu symptoms under 6
Do not give under 2 for any indication

records.population: Australian consumers, including carers of infants and children
verifiedBoundaries: Different child products can have different strengths
Do not combine multiple paracetamol-containing products
Suspected overdose or medicine error requires Poisons Information Centre advice

records.population: People in Australia with suspected poisoning, overdose or medicine error
verifiedBoundaries: Available 24 hours a day throughout Australia
Contact route only, not treatment guidance

records.population: Children and adults with acute cough; 10 child trials
verifiedBoundaries: No good evidence for or against OTC acute-cough medicines
Products and results were heterogeneous
Evidence search ended in March 2014

records.population: Children aged 12 months to 18 years with acute cough
verifiedBoundaries: Mostly one-night exposure
Low-to-moderate certainty
Not for infants up to 12 months

records.population: Adults and children with acute upper-respiratory infection; largest child study aged 6 to 10 years
verifiedBoundaries: Limited evidence
Largest positive child study is older than the core module population
Infant directness unresolved
